name: the primitive type called hung

VINIT.justification: The state of memory before any pointers or objects are instantiated.

TANCH.justification: The final boundary ensuring no element remains unpaired or over-allocated.

AFWD.justification: The directed mapping from a source address to a target value.

AREV.justification: The reverse mapping from a value back to its unique source address.

CLINK.justification: The sequential linking of memory addresses to form a coherent data structure.

IMSCRIB.justification: A node that contains its own address, establishing identity within the heap.

FSPLIT.justification: Splitting a single 1:1 relation into its domain and codomain components.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstituting the domain and codomain back into a single 1:1 stoichiometric pair.

EVALT.justification: The state where every input maps to exactly one output.

EVALF.justification: The state where a mapping is either partial or non-injective.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where a pointer is declared but its target is simultaneously null and pending.

IFIX.justification: The permanent fixation of a 1:1 mapping in read-only memory.

phase_3.void_description: uninitialized memory space without defined stoichiometry
phase_3.false_description: stoichiometric failure where mapping is not 1:1
phase_3.both_description: a superposition of allocation and deallocation during a swap

steps.domain_action: initialize the stoichiometric environment for the hung primitive
identify the base unit of the 1:1 relation
project the unit toward its potential partner
branch the relation to verify both the source and the target
confirm the source is unique and available
link the source to the target address
confirm the target is unique and available
fuse the verified source and target into a single stoichiometric bond
record the 1:1 mapping permanently in the allocation table
close the stoichiometric operation and seal the boundary

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the 1:1 mapping preserves information density without loss or gain
